test1_MV-cover-letter-studio-006_+_NF-fi.summary: Documented artifacts (ManagerLocation, EngineerLocation, ResponsibilitiesBuild, EmployerShare, DirectorLocation, QualificationsBuild, CompensationShare, DepartmentLocation) are correctly DROPPED — the NF-final-PII-001 fix works for the heading-word gluings it enumerated. BUT City/place-name + boilerplate-label CamelCase concatenation artifacts SURVIVE and rank as top chips, including 'SydneySalary' which the acceptance criterion explicitly names as must-not-appear.
test1_MV-cover-letter-studio-006_+_NF-fi.root_cause_inferred: _is_camel_concatenation_artifact() flags a token only when EVERY CamelCase segment is in _ARTIFACT_SPLIT_WORDS; that set holds country names (australia/canada/...) but NOT city names (sydney/melbourne/brisbane/adelaide/perth/canberra). So 'Sydney'+'Salary' fails the all-segments test, survives _is_semantic_keyword, then scores high in _skill_score (+45 internal-uppercase, +25 leading-cap) and ranks into the 10-chip panel (cover_letters.py:161-202,205-228).
test1_MV-cover-letter-studio-006_+_NF-fi.reproduced: 3x (API run1, API run2 fresh session, real-browser Playwright UI) — VERIFIED TWICE+
test1_MV-cover-letter-studio-006_+_NF-fi.adversarial_variants_of_my_design: MelbourneSalary/BrisbaneLocation/AdelaideSalary/PerthSalary/CanberraLocation (city+label) — all broke the filter, beyond the task-named SydneySalary

test2a_single_agent_async_no_resume_refu.summary: coverLetter + tailor async runs for R2 (no resume) each complete the BackgroundJob with status='completed' (NOT 'failed'), result.missingResume=true and an honest user-actionable message; 'MissingResumeError' appears NOWHERE user-visible; quota NOT charged; no operator resume seeded.
test2a_single_agent_async_no_resume_refu.cover_letter_message: Add your resume before generating a cover letter.
test2a_single_agent_async_no_resume_refu.tailor_message: Add your resume before tailoring or generating an application.
test2a_single_agent_async_no_resume_refu.note_agentrun_rows: the internal AgentRun audit rows show status='failed' but their error text is the honest message ('Add your resume...'), never the class name — surfaced via /agents/runs with no leak

test2b_pipeline_async_no_resume_refusal.summary: THE round-1 review gap. POST /agents/pipeline/run for R2 (no resume) -> 202 BackgroundJob; the pipeline refuses at the fitScorer step and the pipeline branch's MissingResumeError handler completes the job with status='completed', missingResume=true, honest message 'Add your resume before scoring jobs against it.', error=None. No 'MissingResumeError' anywhere user-visible; quota not charged. VERIFIED ON PROD.
test2b_pipeline_async_no_resume_refusal.pipeline_message: Add your resume before scoring jobs against it.

new_findings.title: JD Keyword Coverage still surfaces City/place-name + boilerplate-label CamelCase concatenation artifacts (SydneySalary, MelbourneSalary, ...) as top chips
new_findings.repro: As any Pro user, create a job whose scraped JD contains 'ManagerLocation: SydneySalary: $180k ...'; generate a cover letter; GET /cover-letters/{id}/insights -> keywords.items contains 'SydneySalary','MelbourneSalary','BrisbaneLocation'. Reproduced 3x incl. real browser.
new_findings.fix_hint: _is_camel_concatenation_artifact should also flag a token whose CamelCase split yields a place-name segment + a boilerplate/label segment (extend _ARTIFACT_SPLIT_WORDS with common AU/global city names, OR relax the all-segments rule so any boilerplate-label segment paired with a Capitalized non-skill word is dropped), while keeping the _MIXED_CASE_TECH_ALLOWLIST guard so JavaScript/PostgreSQL/etc. remain.

new_findings.title: Cover-letter studio UI silently drops the honest async no-resume refusal message
new_findings.class: adjacent to NF-final-PII-002 (backend fix itself is correct)
new_findings.detail: The backend correctly returns a 'completed' BackgroundJob with result.message='Add your resume before generating a cover letter.' and missingResume=true (no class leak, quota refunded). But apps/web/src/app/dashboard/cover-letters/page.tsx generate() calls load(result.cover_letter_id) [undefined] and setError(null) for the missingResume-completed case, so the honest message is never surfaced — clicking 'Generate Draft' with no resume yields a silent no-op (empty alert, no message, no letter, 0 console errors). Reproduced 2x in real browser.
new_findings.fix_hint: In generate()/regenerate(): if result.missingResume (or !result.cover_letter_id), surface result.message via setError(result.message) (or a dedicated non-error banner) instead of clearing state.

prohibitions_respected: no pytest; no service restarts; no code edits; no force-push; only my 2 accounts touched (+ read-only CLM-027 lookups); never touched sarkar.vikram/admin; all secrets truncated; DB ops single-row WHERE-keyed under search_path=aether with current_schema() asserted, ?schema= stripped via urllib.
